The Policy # or Policy #s for the policy.  If there is an endorsement number included (i.e. endt 90, Endt 79, Correction 85) remove it. Replace any spaces within individual policy numbers near the end (within the last 6 digits or so) with a dash.

The Endorsement # or Endorsement #s for the policy.  If there is an endorsement number already here leave it, and if there is endorsement info in the policy field, move it / concatenate it here (i.e. endt 90, Endt 79, Correction 85) remove it.  If there are multiple endorsements, as indicated by line breaks or other delimiters like /, separate each with a semi-colon.

The effective date of the policy record in question.  If multiple dates are in the field, or there is a date and non-date information, output the earliest date in mm/dd/yyyy format as the effective date.

The Expiration date of the policy record in question.  If multiple dates are in the field, or there is a date and non-date information, output the latest date in the field in mm/dd/yyyy format as the expiration date.

The premium for the policy.  If it is a well formatted number leave as is.  If it appears to be multiple premium values, add them up.  If it shows a billed / collected amount (i.e. $70,000 / $70,000) use the denominator or sum of denominators.  Leave blank if blank, unknown for other cases.
